code: @Manual{,   title = {prolfquasaint: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua},   author = {Witold Wolski},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.5},   url = {https://prolfqua.github.io/prolfquasaint/}, }

what: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua
title: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua
text: prolfquasaint prolfqua integration layer SAINTexpress-based protein interaction scoring. converts prolfqua LFQData objects SAINT input tables, runs SAINTexpress, exposes results prolfqua contrast adapters. 📖 Documentation: https://prolfqua.github.io/prolfquasaint/

title: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua
text: SAINTexpress concerns split across three packages: saintexpress — pure-R implementation spectral-count intensity scoring engines. saintexpressbin — ships native SAINTexpress-spc SAINTexpress-int binaries plus Docker fallback macOS. prolfquasaint (package) — prolfqua integration. Depends saintexpress (Imports); saintexpressbin Suggests. engine =   "binary" requested without saintexpressbin installed, call falls back R engine warning.

title: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua
text: prolfquasaint’s Remotes: field references prolfqua/saintexpress prolfqua/saintexpressbin, pak::pkg_install("prolfqua/prolfquasaint") resolve transitively. dependency resolver walk nested Remotes:, install saintexpress saintexpressbin explicitly first (see TODO/TODO_transitive_remotes.md).
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("prolfqua/saintexpress") remotes::install_github("prolfqua/saintexpressbin")  # optional, for native engine remotes::install_github("fgcz/prolfqua") remotes::install_github("prolfqua/prolfquasaint")

title: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua
text: user-facing surface stable: protein_2localSaint() — convert tidy long-format protein quantification table SAINT inter / prey / bait data frames. runSaint(si, ..., engine = c("binary", "r"), optimizer = c("base", "nloptr")) — run SAINTexpress. engine = "binary" delegates saintexpressbin::saintexpress_run(); engine = "r" delegates saintexpress::run_saint(). ContrastsSAINTexpress, ContrastsSAINTFacade — prolfqua R6 adapters expose SAINT results prolfqua contrast interface.

title: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua
text: inst/test/ holds TIP49 reference dataset comparison harness gates R engine native SAINTexpress 3.6.3 binary. testthat suite includes cross-package comparison: inst/test/run_tip49_comparison.sh regenerates comparison reports (tip49_comparison_363_spc.html matching diff). Use changes either engine refresh -disk evidence.

title: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua
text: native-r-spc-comparison.qmd — spectral-count engine: native binary vs R implementation TIP49 fixture. native-r-int-comparison.qmd — intensity engine: native binary vs R implementation TIP49 fixture. Build make build-vignettes. introductory walk-throughs simulated data, see vignettes shipped saintexpress saintexpressbin.

title: SAINT-Based Protein Interaction Analysis with prolfqua
text: original SAINTexpress 3.6.3 source archive downloaded saint-apms SourceForge project: SourceForge files page: https://sourceforge.net/projects/saint-apms/files/ Archive: SAINTexpress_v3.6.3__2018-03-09.tar.gz SourceForge listing date: 2018-03-15 extracted cleaned C++ source lives separate repository; package depends prebuilt binaries shipped saintexpressbin.

title: SAINTexpress contrast analysis facade — ContrastsSAINTFacade
text: R6 class generator.

title: SAINTexpress contrast analysis facade — ContrastsSAINTFacade
text: Encapsulates SAINTexpress pipeline can reached facade-registry dispatch prolfqua built-methods (lm, limma, firth, ...). Wraps protein_2localSaint -> runSaint -> ContrastsSAINTexpress, bait / control / gene / protein-length columns resolved annotation data attached input LFQData. Differs LM-style facades three ways: contrast set derived bait column (one bait         vs. controls), contrasts argument ignored. output schema uses Bait / log2_EFCs /         SaintScore / BFDR; p.value NA. facade carries SAINT input tables raw         SAINTexpress result via extra_artifacts()         downstream report rendering.

title: SAINTexpress contrast analysis facade — ContrastsSAINTFacade
text: lfqdata aggregated LFQData object bait / control columns annotation modelstr ignored — SAINT derives contrasts bait column; accepted facade uniformity contrasts ignored reason row_annot data.frame protein-level annotation (must contain hierarchy key column). NULL, falls back lfqdata$hierarchy_keys() content long table. spc TRUE run SAINTexpress-spc; default FALSE (intensity model). engine SAINTexpress engine; one "r" (default) whatever runSaint accepts.

title: Wrapper to results produced by SAINTexpress — ContrastsSAINTexpress
code: seout <- prolfqua::prolfqua_data("data_SAINTe_output") cse <- ContrastsSAINTexpress$new(seout$list) stopifnot(dim(cse$to_wide()) == c(64,13)) cse$get_contrast_sides() #>       contrast group_1 group_2 #> 1 b vs Control       b Control #> 2 c vs Control       c Control #> 3 d vs Control       d Control #> 4 e vs Control       e Control stopifnot(dim(cse$get_contrasts()) == c(236,7)) cse$get_linfct() #> NULL pl <- cse$get_Plotter() stopifnot(c("gg", "ggplot") %in% class(pl$volcano()$FDR))

title: set factors and sample names columns — dataset_set_factors_deprecated
code: dataset_csv_examples <- system.file("application/dataset_csv", package = "prolfquapp") files <- dir(dataset_csv_examples, pattern = "*.csv")  res <- list() for (i in seq_along(files)) {   res[[i]] <- readr::read_csv(file.path(dataset_csv_examples, files[i]),     show_col_types = FALSE) }  for (i in seq_along(res)) {   atable <- prolfqua::AnalysisConfiguration$new()   atable$hierarchy[["protein_Id"]] <- c("Protein")   atable$hierarchy[["peptide_Id"]] <- c("Peptide")   tmp <- prolfquasaint::dataset_set_factors_deprecated(atable, res[[i]])   cat(i, " : ", length(tmp$atable$factors), "factors : ",     paste(tmp$atable$factors, collapse = "; "), "\n") }

what: Add protein lengths from fasta file to data frame (id_col - protein id column.) — add_protein_lengths
title: Add protein lengths from fasta file to data frame (id_col - protein id column.) — add_protein_lengths
text: Executes SAINTexpress prepared input data. Delegates saintexpressbin package native binary / Docker engine, saintexpress package pure-R engine. `engine = "binary"` requested saintexpressbin installed, warning issued call falls back R engine.

title: Add protein lengths from fasta file to data frame (id_col - protein id column.) — add_protein_lengths
code: add_protein_lengths(intdata, fasta, id_col = "protein_Id")  protein_2localSaint(   xx,   quantcolumn = "mq.protein.intensity",   proteinID = "protein_Id",   geneNames = proteinID,   proteinLength = "protein.length",   IP_name = "raw.file",   baitCol = "bait",   CorTCol = "CorT" )  runSaint(   si,   filedir = getwd(),   spc = TRUE,   CLEANUP = TRUE,   use_docker = NULL,   engine = c("binary", "r"),   optimizer = c("base", "nloptr") )

title: Add protein lengths from fasta file to data frame (id_col - protein id column.) — add_protein_lengths
text: intdata data.frame fasta list sequences created read.fasta id_col column protein ids/accessions. xx data.frame long format quantcolumn intensity column proteinID protein accession geneNames column gene names proteinLength column protein lengths IP_name raw.file baitCol column bait definition (condition) CorTCol control TRUE (SaintExpress speach) si output protein_2localSaint function filedir store saint express inputs spc TRUE spectral counts, FALSE intensities (see SAINTexpress documentation) CLEANUP TRUE remove files generated SAINTexpress use_docker logical NULL. NULL (default) uses native executable available falls back Docker macOS. TRUE forces Docker. FALSE forces native execution. engine execution engine. "binary" runs bundled SAINTexpress via saintexpressbin; "r" runs R implementation via saintexpress. optimizer optimizer engine = "r". "base" uses optim; "nloptr" uses NLopt COBYLA nloptr installed.

title: Add protein lengths from fasta file to data frame (id_col - protein id column.) — add_protein_lengths
text: `intdata` added `protein.length` column. named list `inter`, `prey`, `bait` SAINT input tables. list SAINTexpress `listFile`, parsed `list`, run ``.

title: Add protein lengths from fasta file to data frame (id_col - protein id column.) — add_protein_lengths
code: add_protein_lengths(   data.frame(protein_Id = "P1"),   list(P1 = "MPEPTIDE") ) #>   protein_Id protein.length #> 1         P1              8   bb <- prolfqua::prolfqua_data('data_IonstarProtein_subsetNorm') bb$config <- bb$config$clone(deep = TRUE) xx <- prolfqua::LFQData$new(bb$data, bb$config) exampleDat <- xx$data_long() |>   dplyr::mutate(CorT = dplyr::case_when(dilution. == "a" ~ "C", TRUE ~ "T")) # sample protein lengths  tmp <- data.frame(protein_Id = unique(exampleDat$protein_Id)) tmp$proteinLength <- as.integer(runif(nrow(tmp), min = 150, max = 2500)) exampleDat <- dplyr::inner_join(tmp, exampleDat) #> Joining with `by = join_by(protein_Id)` res <- protein_2localSaint(exampleDat,quantcolumn = "medpolish",                    proteinID = "protein_Id",                    proteinLength = "proteinLength",                    IP_name = "raw.file",                    baitCol = "dilution.",                    CorTCol = "CorT"                    )  stopifnot(names(res) == c( "inter", "prey",  "bait"))
